name: Director Schmector

attacks.name: 360° laser burst
attacks.tell: The Director extends both arms and spins quickly, emitting a laser ring around him.
attacks.howToAvoid: Jump over the laser when it passes by your position (or crouch if the laser goes high, depending on the height of the emitter)
attacks.notes: It covers everything around, you have to have timing. Causes severe energy damage if connected

attacks.name: platform attack
attacks.tell: Schmector activates a panel and electrifies sections of the arena floor (they flash red before)
attacks.howToAvoid: Immediately leave the tiles glowing red before they receive the shock
attacks.notes: Environmental Trap: Ground shocks deal high damage and stun. Director takes advantage of those moments to attack you

attacks.name: ORC Call
attacks.tell: He presses his watch and various O.R.C signals. they sound
attacks.howToAvoid: N/A; Get ready for O.R.C robots. entering the fight
attacks.notes: Summons multiple O.R.C units. (stronger variants of TAYZ.T, etc.) to assist you. Manage crowd besides the boss

attacks.name: pulse discharge
attacks.tell: Aim your cannon arm and fire a blue electric pulse projectile
attacks.howToAvoid: Side Dodge; The projectile is fast but you can see it coming because of the load on your arm
attacks.notes: If the pulse hits you, it drains some of your stamina in addition to damaging

bossPhases.label: Phase 1
bossPhases.summary: Schmector opens with energy shots and direct pressure, still without fully weaponizing the room.
bossPhases.trigger: It begins when the central button is pressed in the chamber beneath Castle Moldorc.

attacks.name: Quick Pulse Shot
attacks.tell: He aims the cannon arm and fires a blue pulse with almost no charge time.
attacks.howToAvoid: Move sideways as soon as you see the arm line up with your chest.
attacks.notes: This is the tool Schmector uses to keep microstamina pressure on you throughout the fight.

attacks.name: Charged Pulse Shot
attacks.tell: The arm glows brighter before releasing a much heavier pulse.
attacks.howToAvoid: Do not block it on autopilot; gain an angle and avoid giving him a big hit.
attacks.notes: If it lands, your room to recover in the phase drops sharply.

bossPhases.label: Phase 2
bossPhases.summary: The static-charge turrets come online and the fight makes it clear you are battling the lab as much as Schmector himself.
bossPhases.trigger: It enters at 80% health with the static-charge turrets powering up.
newPatterns: The turrets carve up floor space and especially punish anyone trying to heal without relocating.

attacks.name: Static Charge Turrets
attacks.tell: The side turrets charge the floor with periodic shocks.
attacks.howToAvoid: Do not watch only Schmector; read the room and rotate before you get pinned between live zones.
attacks.notes: They are the basis for all of the boss’s dangerous overlaps.

attacks.name: Charged Pulse Shot
attacks.tell: Schmector uses the turret distraction to line up a more serious shot.
attacks.howToAvoid: Move your feet first and think about punishing second; staying pinned is the real loss here.
attacks.notes: The isolated damage is not the worst part; the worst part is taking it while a turret closes your escape.

bossPhases.label: Phase 3
bossPhases.summary: PROJECT O.R.C. enters the fight. Schmector starts summoning O.R.C. Weaver Jrs. and you can no longer treat adds as a side detail.
bossPhases.trigger: It enters at 60% health when he announces PROJECT O.R.C.
newPatterns: The O.R.C. spiders can keep appearing throughout the phase if the pattern repeats.

attacks.name: Project O.R.C. Call
attacks.tell: He taps his watch and opens the summon pattern for O.R.C. spiders.
attacks.howToAvoid: If the room is already crowded, separate the spiders before going back to the boss.
attacks.notes: This mechanic completely changes the fight’s priority order.

attacks.name: Quick Pulse Shot
attacks.tell: He abuses your attention on the adds to keep firing light pressure shots.
attacks.howToAvoid: Do not hand him a straight angle while repositioning around the spiders.
attacks.notes: The real problem in this phase is visual chaos, not a single damage number.

bossPhases.label: Phase 4
bossPhases.summary: The lab adds the green lasers. From here on you are no longer fighting one threat at a time, but several systems trying to close the room at once.
bossPhases.trigger: It enters at 40% health when he activates the green laser protocol.
newPatterns: Playing near the back of the room opposite the entrance greatly reduces the laser sweep risk.

attacks.name: Green Laser Sweep
attacks.tell: Two pairs of green lasers cross the room and begin sweeping the floor.
attacks.howToAvoid: Respect the open lane and do not try to heal where the next sweep is already going to catch you.
attacks.notes: This is the point where room awareness matters more than any long combo.

attacks.name: Project O.R.C. Call
attacks.tell: Summons can still appear while the lasers remain active.
attacks.howToAvoid: Do not try to clean everything at once; prioritize the opening that lets you survive the next cycle.
attacks.notes: The phase punishes greed more than low damage output.

bossPhases.label: Phase 5
bossPhases.summary: The finish stacks everything: static-charge turrets, O.R.C. Weaver Jrs., and green lasers. The real objective here is not losing the room’s mental rhythm.
bossPhases.trigger: It enters at 20% health when he activates all emergency procedures.
newPatterns: The ending rewards clean burst on the boss if you see a real opening; dragging it out usually makes the fight worse.

attacks.name: Emergency Overlap
attacks.tell: Schmector can now trigger any previous mechanic while the others are still live.
attacks.howToAvoid: Do not play reactively and late; decide ahead of time which pattern you will respect first.
attacks.notes: The final difficulty comes from the combination, not from a single new move.

attacks.name: Green Laser Sweep
attacks.tell: The lasers keep sweeping while the rest of the hazards remain active.
attacks.howToAvoid: Think in short, clean routes; crossing half the room in panic usually gets you killed.
attacks.notes: If this mechanic steals your attention, the turrets or spiders finish the mistake.

description: Dalton Schmector, head of Ominent and the mind behind Project O.R.C., is the secret final boss of the game. He hides beneath Castle Moldorc and is one of the hardest non-repeatable fights.
behavior: It starts similarly to the Assistant Manager, but every phase changes the room's main hazard: static-charge turrets, O.R.C summons, and green lasers that all start overlapping in the final stretch.
interactionWithPlayer: He is completely hostile and required for the good ending. His whole kit applies microstamina pressure, so even smaller hits keep shrinking your margin for error.
interactionWithCreatures: He summons O.R.C. Weaver Jrs and uses the lab's automated defenses as an extension of his own attack pattern.
strategy: Sour and busting are the cleanest answers. The fight is won by learning the phase order and preserving room awareness for the final stretch, where he can stack turrets, O.R.C spiders, and lasers together.
respawnInfo: Does not respawn. It can only be defeated once per save.

combatStats.attackDamageSummary:
Quick shot: 100
Charged shot: 160
Small turret shots: 100
Corner turret shots: 160
Overload shock: 100
Green laser: 30
